rules.pattern: any RHB submodel launch sequence
rules.action: before every ac_driver.run_inference(), strobe PL[0x00] with 0x100 to clear PL[0x20].wr_done; do not rely on run-repeat warmup for correctness
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer and CSPN app runners use clear_wr_done to avoid first-frame/stale-output pollution without run-repeat=2

rules.pattern: packed deployment config.yaml
rules.action: set rram_only=false unless a specific package has board-pass evidence with rram_only=true
rules.evidence: Earlier minimal Linear tests failed on rram_only=true; accepted CompletionFormer and CSPN packages use the non-rram-only path

rules.pattern: RHB submodel with multiple output tensors
rules.action: split fan-out into one RHB submodel per output and do fan-out/concat glue on Host
rules.evidence: RHB board runner accepts one output tensor per submodel; multi-output CSPN probes failed until split

rules.pattern: image-like Conv2d submodel in NCHW/BCHW tensors
rules.action: use layout input0=BCHW for CSPN-style image tensors; only use BWC for packages already proven with BWC
rules.evidence: CSPN stagewise v3 hostsample package and board app use BCHW image-like submodels

rules.pattern: any RHB region or packed submodel
rules.action: split regions before packing when packed weights approach the 8MB limit; prefer measured packer artifact size over FP32 parameter count
rules.evidence: AGENT-flow v2 deployment target is RHB 8MB; successful packages keep each launched submodel under budget

rules.pattern: Conv2d(k=1 or k=3, stride=1) with optional BN-fold and ReLU in validated spatial/channel range
rules.action: prefer large contiguous Conv/ReLU blocks on RHB, but require compile+cmodel+board and real-feature boundary validation before promotion
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer dec6-dec3, dec2 exact blocks, dep/gd/cf conv heads; CSPN stagewise conv blocks in accepted hostsample runner

rules.pattern: ConvTranspose2d upsample
rules.action: replace with Host resize plus RHB Conv2d only for hardware-aligned variants that are retrained or explicitly accepted
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer HW128 successful path uses resize+conv decoder rather than transposed convolution

rules.pattern: Conv2d stride=2 downsample that causes timeout or accuracy failure
rules.action: for retrainable aligned models, replace stride2 spatial Conv with Host sample/downsample plus RHB 1x1 Conv; keep sample/gather outside the RHB graph
rules.evidence: CSPN HW128 stagewise v3 hostsample uses Host sample + RHB 1x1 style downsample to avoid stride2/small-space failures
rules.risk: semantic change unless the aligned model is trained with this structure

rules.pattern: Conv2d with high input-channel pressure or full-res channel pressure
rules.action: split input channels into RHB partial Conv chunks; place bias in one chunk or add it once on Host; dequantize/sum/requantize with recorded activation scales
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer dec2 up-conv input-channel chunks and CSPN partial Conv experiments show exact split is the safe fallback for channel pressure
rules.risk: medium; launch count and scale glue must be budgeted

rules.pattern: Conv2d with high output-channel pressure but independent output channels
rules.action: split output channels into separate RHB submodels and concatenate outputs on Host; this preserves Conv semantics exactly
rules.evidence: CSPN high-channel diagnostics used OC split as a board-safe way to reduce per-launch pressure
rules.risk: medium; boundary and launch overhead may dominate

rules.pattern: Conv2d at H/W<=8 with C_in or C_out>=16, especially 8x8 16/32-channel regions
rules.action: do not deploy monolithically; use IC/OC split, smaller block boundary, or Host fallback; accept only after board pass
rules.evidence: CSPN stage4/downsample probes repeatedly showed status wait timeout or stale-output behavior for small-space high-channel convs

rules.pattern: Slice/Gather/indexing used for spatial downsample inside a candidate RHB graph
rules.action: perform sampling/gather on Host and feed the sampled tensor to a RHB Conv submodel; do not keep downsample Slice inside RHB
rules.evidence: CSPN stagewise hostsample runner moved sampling outside RHB and uses pre-sampled inputs

rules.pattern: Concat, residual Add, split-sum, fan-in/fan-out boundary glue
rules.action: perform boundary glue on Host unless the exact fused submodel has board and real-feature accuracy evidence
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer and CSPN accepted runners keep concat/sum/requant/residual glue on Host to preserve scale contracts
rules.risk: low accuracy risk, medium latency risk

rules.pattern: Sigmoid or confidence-map sigmoid
rules.action: run preceding Conv on RHB and apply true sigmoid on Host
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer cf_dec0 hardware Sigmoid lowered incorrectly; Host sigmoid is part of the accepted conv-only confidence-head pipeline
rules.risk: high if run on RHB without fresh proof

rules.pattern: GELU in Transformer/PVT MLP
rules.action: use ReLU approximation only in a hardware-aligned model that will be retrained; otherwise keep on Host or probe a token-friendly submodel
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer PVT experiments used ReLU as aligned approximation; accepted depth app path does not rely on unvalidated GELU-on-RHB

rules.pattern: tensor-tensor gate multiply, CBAM channel/spatial gate multiply, generic Mul with dynamic tensor inputs
rules.board_status: not accepted in successful final graphs
rules.action: run gate multiply on Host unless the exact shape has board-pass evidence; conv/linear parts of gates may be considered separately
rules.evidence: CBAM gate multiply was not retained in the accepted CompletionFormer/CSPN RHB schedules

rules.pattern: fuse independent depth/guidance/confidence or dep/gd/cf heads into one wide submodel
rules.action: keep heads as separate submodels with independent activation scales unless fused candidate passes layerwise and end-to-end validation
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer fused dep/gd/cf dec1 increased error; user explicitly rejected fused-head path due to quantization error

rules.pattern: Host/RHB boundary with int8 activation handoff
rules.action: record per-submodel input/output scales; implement dequant/requant glue explicitly; do not apply runner-only calibration to a qscale=1 packer unless the compiled wrapper implements the same scale contract
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer 32-sample calibration and CSPN stagewise scale-aware runner use explicit boundary scales; runner-only scale on old CSPN qscale=1 packages was rejected

rules.pattern: board output differs from CModel/Host despite compile+cmodel pass
rules.action: run real-feature layerwise board-vs-host probes; estimate effective output scale/ratio stability across calibration samples before accepting scale compensation
rules.evidence: CSPN head and stage diagnostics showed compile/cmodel pass can hide board effective-scale compression

rules.pattern: small final task prediction head after stable RHB feature tensor
rules.action: score Host-head and RHB-head allocations; use Host when it recovers task metric or avoids unstable single-output Conv compression
rules.evidence: CSPN diagnostics found tail heads are high-sensitivity; accepted app keeps the board-proven padded16/hostsample path but framework must consider Host heads as accuracy-first fallback
rules.risk: low accuracy risk, medium latency risk

rules.pattern: final Conv2d(C -> 1, kernel=3, padding=1) with dynamic range compression
rules.action: pad output channels to 16 and take channel0 only when representative end-to-end validation beats Host or partial-sum alternatives; do not treat as universal exact fix
rules.evidence: CSPN current app uses padded16 final depth head as the accepted board demo path; earlier scalar gain/fakequant/QAT004 variants were superseded

rules.pattern: new accepted schedule or rewritten allocation
rules.action: run compile, cmodel, board smoke, real-feature layerwise comparison, and end-to-end metric visualization before promoting into successful_pipelines
rules.evidence: Both CompletionFormer and CSPN success paths were accepted only after app-visible board/ref/gt visualization and sample-set metrics

rules.pattern: any cmodel/board-vs-PyTorch or quantization-error attribution
rules.action: before attributing error to quantization, verify that the compiled ONNX initializers match a fresh export from the current Python model/checkpoint; record model source timestamp or hash, checkpoint path/hash, ONNX path/hash, and packer path
rules.evidence: NLSPN id_dec1_partial_oc0_64_ic0_64: old ONNX vs current export weight corr -0.0038028945, current export vs current Python corr 1.0. Existing cmodel output was explained by old ONNX weights with corr 0.9999436, while current Python quantized output had corr -0.00456717 against cmodel.
rules.risk: critical; stale ONNX/packer artifacts masquerade as quantization or saturation error

rules.pattern: ModulatedDeformConvFunction / custom deformable convolution / dynamic offset sampling
rules.action: keep NLSPN propagation on Host; if full RHB is required, replace with retrainable fixed-neighbor shift/sum propagation
rules.evidence: NLSPN_ECCV20 propagation calls ModulatedDeformConvFunction.apply with dynamic offset/mask; no accepted CompletionFormer/CSPN board demo uses dynamic deformable sampling

rules.pattern: LeakyReLU(negative_slope=0.2)
rules.action: replace with ReLU only in a retrained hardware-aligned model; otherwise keep activation on Host or export Conv-only boundary
rules.evidence: NLSPN_ECCV20 common.py uses LeakyReLU in Conv/decoder blocks; current accepted RHB rules are ReLU/Conv-centric

rules.pattern: large spatial Conv2d with high input channels, e.g. 128x128 and IC >= 128
rules.action: split input channels into 64-channel no-bias/no-activation partial Conv submodels; Host sums partial outputs then applies bias/BN/ReLU glue
rules.evidence: NLSPN dec2/head monolithic Conv passed compile/cmodel but failed board; nlspn_hw_*_partial_ic64 probes pass board All same True

rules.pattern: post-sum ReLU or folded BN+ReLU after input-channel partial Conv accumulation
rules.action: represent post-sum bias/BN/ReLU as 1x1 affine Conv + ReLU RHB block after Host partial-output sum
rules.evidence: NLSPN postsum_identity_relu_64 and postsum_affine_relu_64 pass board All same True; standalone postsum_relu_64 fails compile

rules.pattern: one-channel post-sum affine+ReLU head
rules.action: pad 1-channel post-sum tensor to 8 channels, run 1x1 affine+ReLU on RHB, then Host slices channel 0
rules.evidence: NLSPN postsum_affine_relu_1 packer fails; postsum_affine_relu_8 passes board All same True

rules.pattern: input-channel-split Conv partials summed on Host, followed by BN/ReLU/affine post submodel on RHB
rules.action: estimate both partial output scales and the downstream post-submodel input_scale using real-feature board traces; do not accept per-submodel output_scale alone when the Host partial-sum feeds BN/ReLU/post
rules.evidence: NLSPN id_dec1 partial-sum -> post BN/ReLU: limited effective scales gave val32 pred_l1 mean 0.3661 and sample00 pred_init_l1 0.7006. Enabling id_dec1_partial_ic0 corrected output scale alone worsened sample00 pred_l1 to 0.9365. Jointly setting id_dec1_partial_ic0 corrected_output_scale=7.20130917 and id_dec1_post input_scale=4.0 improved val32 pred_l1 mean to 0.1397, worst to 0.2101, sample00 pred_init_l1 to 0.2678.
rules.risk: high if omitted for prediction-init or guidance heads

rules.pattern: RHB prediction-init output feeding Host propagation or downstream depth refinement
rules.action: use affine only as a diagnostic upper bound unless the equivalent operation is added to the compiler-aligned model graph and retrained/validated; do not silently inject runtime affine into default deployment
rules.evidence: NLSPN val32 diagnostic affine improved pred_l1 mean to 0.101815, but user rejected manual compensation. Default compiler-aligned path uses pred-init/guidance scale-only contract with pred_l1 mean 0.137466 and max 0.185438.
rules.risk: high if used as hidden runtime compensation; medium if model-owned and retrained

rules.pattern: NLSPN HW128 default scale-only deployment, pred_init/guidance/confidence heads
rules.action: when optimizing NLSPN, first inspect fd2 -> id_dec1 -> pred_init boundary scales and saturation; confidence logits may have large absolute error but pred_init dominates final depth error
rules.evidence: val32 oracle ablation: default pred L1 mean 0.137466; oracle pred_init reduces to 0.025359; oracle guidance only to 0.116290; oracle confidence only to 0.122851
rules.risk: high if optimizing the wrong head first

rules.pattern: manual post-board pred_init affine compensation
rules.action: do not use hidden runner affine as default; if the correction is needed, express it as a model-owned graph layer or train boundary-aware scale/QAT so checkpoint semantics include it
rules.evidence: manual pred_init affine reduced val32 pred L1 mean from 0.137466 to 0.101815, but user rejected non-model-owned compensation

rules.pattern: NLSPN pred_init fused id_dec1+id_dec0 or full 128-input id_dec0 at 128x128
rules.action: keep current 64-channel split for pred_init/id_dec1 unless a new compiler build proves the fused graph; do not promote large fused heads based on ONNX export alone
rules.evidence: nlspn_ckpt_pred_init_fused_iddec1_iddec0 and nlspn_ckpt_pred_init_full_iddec0 both segfaulted in compile.py on 2026-07-13

rules.pattern: NLSPN id_dec1 128x128 Conv3x3 split into 32-input-channel partials
rules.action: do not use 32-channel partial split for id_dec1 on the current compiler; the proven fallback remains 64-channel partial split plus real-feature scale validation
rules.evidence: all four id_dec1 partial32 wrappers ic0_32/ic32_64/ic64_96/ic96_128 segfaulted in compile.py

rules.pattern: NLSPN id_dec1 128x128 Conv3x3 split into 32-output-channel partials plus per-OC post BN/ReLU
rules.action: do not use OC32 split for id_dec1 on the current compiler; keep the proven IC64 split and optimize scale/model structure around that boundary
rules.evidence: oc0_32/oc32_64 wrappers for id_dec1_partial_ic0/ic64 and id_dec1_post_bn_relu all segfaulted in compile.py on 2026-07-13

rules.pattern: NLSPN id_dec1_partial_oc0_64_ic0_64 board raw output
rules.action: measure raw int8 saturation and raw-to-reference effective scale per calibration set before changing downstream heads; treat saturation above about 2% mean or 5% max as a primary accuracy risk
rules.evidence: val32 board traces: id_dec1_partial_ic0 raw saturation mean 0.021871, max 0.074828, L1 mean 15.987814, corr 0.952933; ic64_128 counterpart sat mean 0.000023 and corr 0.998668

rules.pattern: scalar raw-to-reference affine on id_dec1_partial_oc0_64_ic0_64 output
rules.action: do not promote scalar output affine as the default fix; use it only to estimate an upper bound and then prefer model-owned structure, QAT, or compiler-supported split changes
rules.evidence: val4 board test: default pred L1 mean 0.124362; scalar alpha+bias 0.122154; alpha-only 0.124243. pred_init L1 did not improve: default 0.237483, alpha+bias 0.238874, alpha-only 0.245198

rules.pattern: NLSPN pred_init model-owned 1x1 calibrator trained with GT loss
rules.action: do not expect a single model-owned pred_init affine layer trained on GT to correct board-output error; use board-output-aware deployment calibrator or deeper boundary/QAT around fd2->id_dec1->pred_init
rules.evidence: remote A100 cached batch fine-tune e40: baseline val_l1 0.067286, best val_l1 0.066371, learned weight 1.002364 and bias 0.002158; fixed board affine 0.8865/0.4526 worsened ref GT metrics

rules.pattern: Python model checkpoint, exported ONNX, compiled cmodel output, and packed board artifact
rules.action: before diagnosing board accuracy, verify current Python export matches the ONNX artifact and rebuild compile/cmodel/packer if the checkpoint or wrapper changed; stale ONNX/package comparisons are invalid
rules.evidence: NLSPN 2026-07-13: stale ONNX vs current Python weight corr was -0.0038 and current Python vs stale cmodel corr was -0.0046. Fresh re-export recovered ONNX-vs-Python L1 0.0 and fresh cmodel-vs-current-Python corr 0.999944.

rules.pattern: multi-submodel packer with many Conv submodels sharing one params_wei_ccode transfer
rules.action: keep each board-loaded packer below the validated safe range, currently 24 similar Conv submodels or about 0.9MB params_wei_ccode; split larger groups and route submodels by prefix in the runner
rules.evidence: NLSPN dec5 fresh packer probe: group2/group4/group8/group16/group24 all passed deploy.py All same for dec5_partial_oc0_64_ic0_64; group32 and group36 failed All same without timeout. group24 params_wei_ccode was 884768 bytes; group32 was 1179680 bytes. Splitting dec5 into dec5_a24 and dec5_b12 made representative submodels pass.

rules.pattern: NLSPN split head partial Conv outputs feeding Host partial-sum glue and RHB post-bias/ReLU
rules.action: after compile+cmodel pass, run real-feature board trace and fit per-boundary output_scale from raw int8 to float reference for the partial outputs that materially affect final pred; do not rely only on absmax output_scale when raw dynamic range is compressed
rules.evidence: NLSPN sample0 2026-07-13: fixing id_dec1_partial_ic0 input_scale from 8.507 to 2.0, pred_init partial output scales 23.592->17.977 and 16.331->12.511, and guidance partial output scales 6.258->1.961 and 34.372->24.980 reduced final board-vs-ref pred L1 from 1.285 to 0.0938. The same scale contract on val32 achieved mean pred L1 0.077874 and median 0.060718, versus older val32 configurations around 0.137-0.140 mean L1. Confidence logits output-scale fitting reduced logit L1 but did not improve final pred, so oracle ranking must decide whether to promote a scale fix.

rules.pattern: NLSPN pred_init_partial_ic0 input scale under split-dec5 scale contract
rules.action: keep pred_init_partial_ic0 input_scale=24 for the current val32 deployment; do not lower it to 22, 20, or 18 to chase outlier samples unless a broader calibration run proves improvement

rules.pattern: new model with unsupported or risky compiler pattern
rules.action: first search for mathematically exact compiler-aligned rewrites such as IC/OC split, single-output split, pad-and-slice, Host gather plus RHB 1x1, or Host partial-sum plus RHB affine/ReLU; if software equivalence is exact, it may be used at inference without retraining
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer dec2 split, CSPN/NLSPN IC64 partial Conv, and pad8/pad16 head wrappers preserve model semantics when the Host glue implements the exact scale and bias contract
rules.risk: low if equivalence and board validation are recorded

rules.pattern: rewrite changes mathematical semantics, e.g. GELU->ReLU, ConvTranspose->resize+Conv, stride/downsample Conv->Host sample+1x1, fixed-neighbor replacement for dynamic propagation
rules.action: do not silently apply approximate rewrites in the runner; express the change in the compiler-aligned model source and train or QAT-validate a checkpoint before deployment
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer HW128 and CSPN HW128 accepted paths are compiler-aligned model variants; hidden runtime affine compensation was rejected for NLSPN unless it becomes model-owned

rules.pattern: trained compiler-aligned model before compile/board promotion
rules.action: run software-side RHB-format quant diagnostics on calibration32/64/128 features: absmax/p99/p99.9 scale, QDQ error, saturation rate, kurtosis, skewness, and top-0.1% energy; classify high-risk boundaries before board search
rules.evidence: NLSPN pred_init/id_dec1 failures were dominated by boundary scale, saturation, and outlier behavior that compile/cmodel did not expose; CompletionFormer benefited from per-boundary scale-aware calibration

rules.pattern: automatic Host/RHB allocation for SPN-style depth completion models
rules.action: greedily expand candidate RHB regions across Conv/ReLU/BN-fold/token-friendly blocks until a rule, single-output limit, packed-size limit, quant-risk gate, or board failure stops expansion; then score by measured latency and boundary count
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer became practical only after reducing full-res decoder round trips; CSPN/NLSPN need the same largest-valid-subgraph search instead of tiny layer-by-layer launching
rules.risk: medium; largest compile-pass region still requires board and real-feature validation

rules.pattern: CSPN/NLSPN propagation with dynamic neighbor weights, normalization, shift/pad/index, deformable offset, or iterative update
rules.action: keep propagation on Host unless an exact decomposition into supported RHB submodels plus Host glue is proven; approximate fixed-neighbor or simplified propagation requires retraining
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer offloads Conv-heavy decoding while keeping unsupported glue on Host; NLSPN deform propagation remains Host in the accepted workload split
rules.risk: high if forced into RHB without exact validation

rules.pattern: compiler-aligned model with approximate rewrites requiring retraining on remote GPU server
rules.action: use AgentFlow remote-train profiles to stage code, launch nohup training over SSH, monitor logs, and fetch checkpoints; after fetch, fresh-export ONNX and rebuild compile/cmodel/packer before any board accuracy claim
rules.evidence: CSPN and NLSPN aligned models are trained on the remote CSPN workspace via ssh -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519 -o IdentitiesOnly=yes -p 2222 root@100.104.115.57; stale ONNX/package artifacts previously caused false quantization diagnoses
rules.risk: high if ckpt, ONNX, and packer are not tied together

rules.pattern: multiple validated RHB submodels scheduled by one runner
rules.action: after individual submodels pass board validation, first probe all-in-one only to discover pack/load/offset limits; then search the largest board-exact partition with real runner outputs. Compare debug npz tensors against the accepted baseline when the baseline is trusted, or against reference outputs when the previous board baseline is known to be stale-state polluted. Reject any faster package that changes checked tensors.
rules.evidence: 2026-07-14 three-model sweep: CSPN all-in-one reduced wall latency to 2099.005 ms but changed depth_float/s2_float, so the accepted board-exact partition remains 2-load at 2608.920 ms vs 17147.321 ms baseline. CompletionFormer ckpt00059 already uses one 14-submodel packer and reran at 1481.980 ms tracked latency, so no further load-count gain exists. NLSPN all-in-one 89-submodel pack failed at params_wei offset 8232 out of 13-bit field range; dec5+dec4 62-submodel pack ended at 8160 lines, ran as a 2-pack pipeline, reduced tracked latency 5692.214 -> 4468.575 ms, and improved pred mean_abs vs ref 1.286738 -> 0.136143 by avoiding previous packer-switch state pollution.
rules.risk: high if pack/load success is accepted without tensor/ref comparison or offset headroom checks

rules.pattern: Model-Packer multi-submodel bundle accumulating params_wei offsets
rules.action: during bundle search, reject candidate packers whose generated params_wei offset would exceed the instruction field range; keep practical headroom below the observed failing offset 8232 lines and prefer validated packages at or below 8160 lines unless future hardware/runtime evidence expands the limit
rules.evidence: NLSPN 2026-07-14: 89-submodel all-in-one failed in Model-Packer with Value 8232 out of range for bits 298:286. dec5+dec4 62-submodel pack succeeded with last submodel offset 8144 and total params_wei lines 8160.
rules.risk: high if ignored; packer may fail or generate invalid instructions

rules.pattern: board latency benchmark or CPU-vs-RHB comparison
rules.action: report median or outlier-filtered mean as the primary latency; exclude runtime spikes from headline CPU-vs-RHB ratios; always list excluded samples/runs with the slow stage or rejection reason. Use at least 3 repeats/samples when possible, and never use a single spike caused by status polling, DMA/cache synchronization, or submodel-switch state as the main latency number.
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer val32 had a stable median around 1486.7 ms but a sample31 spike at 3542.147 ms; a later single rerun had dec4_rhb spike to 1019.741 ms and total 2492.185 ms while normal dec4_rhb is about 7-10 ms. Current stable report excludes these outliers and uses CompletionFormer 1486.807 ms, CSPN 2121.742 ms, and NLSPN 4282.089 ms.
rules.risk: medium if ignored; latency comparisons become dominated by board/runtime jitter instead of model allocation quality

rules.pattern: run_inference latency spike where driver log stalls between 'Running inference/Sending inputs' and 'Sending data to accelerator'
rules.action: before board benchmark/inference, clear wr_done before every submodel, set CPU frequency to max, and call mlockall when running as root. Keep CPU affinity and SCHED_FIFO as explicit debug options only, because they can reduce one spike while slowing full-resolution transfer/head stages.
rules.evidence: CompletionFormer 2026-07-14 dec4 outlier stalled about 1013 ms before DMA send; wr_done loops and sta_npu_opt_cnt were normal after send. Full stabilization with CPU3 affinity/SCHED_FIFO removed the dec4 spike but increased total latency to 2028.318 ms. Preferred mitigation --lock-cpu-max-freq --mlockall gave dec4_rhb=7.570 ms and total=1492.605 ms.
rules.risk: medium if skipped; runtime jitter can be misdiagnosed as model/packer latency
